sections.paragraph:
fut. יֶעְתַּר-

(1) i.q. קָטַר to burn incense to a god (Syr. ܥܛܰܪ to smoke with perfume, ܥܶܛܪܐܳ incense; Arab. عطر to breathe odours). Hence עָתָר No. 1.

(2) to pray as a suppliant, to pray to a god (the prayers of the godly being compared to incense; comp. μνημόσυνον τῆς προσευχῆς, Tob_12:12 Act 10:4); to supplicate, to intreat, followed by. לְ and אֶל Gen 25:21 Exo 8:26 10:18 Job 33:26.

Niphal, to let oneself be supplicated by any one (followed by a dat.), to hear and answer, Gen 25:21, וַיֵּעָתֶר לֹו יְהֹוָה “and Jehovah heard and answered him;” 2Sa 21:14 Isa 19:22. Inf. absol. נַעְתֹּור 1Ch 5:20.

Hiphil, i.q. Kal, Exo 8:25 10:17 . Followed by לְ and בְּעַד to be intreated for any one, Exo 8:5, 24 .

Derivative, עָתָר.

II.

עתר

i.q. Chald. עֲתַר (Hebrew עָשַׁר) to be abundant; not used in Kal.

Niphal. id. Pro 27:6, “abundant (i.e. many, frequent) are the kisses of an enemy.” Opp. to, “faithful are the wounds of a friend.”

Hiphil, to multiply, to accumulate, Eze 35:13, וְהַעְתַּרְתֶּם עָלַי דִּבְרֵיכֶם “you have multiplied against me words”, sc. impudent, impious words. Compare גָּדֹול No. 2.

Derivatives, עֲתֶרֶת and pr.n. עֶתֶר.
